data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp uses a microservices architecture to handle different functionalities such as messaging, VoIP, etc., enabling independent deployment and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed into several independent services that communicate over APIs.

data.title: Real-Time Messaging Protocols
data.description: Foundation of WhatsApp's instantaneous messaging feature, ensuring messages are delivered in real-time.
data.technical_spec: Uses XMPP protocol with optimized modifications for efficient message delivery.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: Security architecture ensuring all messages are encrypted between sender and receiver, preventing interceptions.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes the Signal Protocol for encryption.

data.title: Data Replication and Redundancy
data.description: Ensures high availability and fault tolerance with data replication across multiple data centers.
data.technical_spec: Multi-data center topology with automated failover mechanisms.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: High demand capacity handled through cloud-native solutions and scalable resources.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes cloud infrastructure for dynamic scaling, possibly employing Kubernetes or similar orchestration tools.
